quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget's "$2,392.00 (33.4% of total) from just 16 units" and West "trails at $1,414.82 (19.8%), 35.0% below North."

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a product, region, and figure, e.g. "Push Gadget in the West, where its revenue is only $299.00 versus $747.50 in North."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like Sprocket selling the most units (238) yet yielding only 15.8% of revenue and West being "last in three of four product lines."

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget's '33.4% of total', Sprocket's '$4.75 unit price', and West being '35.0% below North.'

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "West trails at $1,414.82 (19.8%), 35.0% below North" and "Gadget leads revenue at $2,392.00 (33.4% of total) from just 16 units."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product, region, and figures, e.g. "Push Gadget in the West, where its revenue is only $299.00 versus $747.50 in North."
